heading: Use of funds for youth workforce investment activities
label: 3164 Use of funds for youth workforce investment activities

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To be eligible to participate in activities carried out under this subpart during any program year an individual shall, at the time the eligibility determination is made, be an out-of-school youth or an in-school youth.

children.label: i
children.text: not attending any school (as defined under State law);

children.label: ii
children.text: not younger than age 16 or older than age 24; and

children.label: I
children.text: A school dropout.

children.label: II
children.text: A youth who is within the age of compulsory school attendance, but has not attended school for at least the most recent complete school year calendar quarter.

children.label: aa
children.text: basic skills deficient; or

children.label: bb
children.text: an English language learner.

children.label: IV
children.text: An individual who is subject to the juvenile or adult justice system.

children.label: V
children.text: A homeless individual (as defined in ), a homeless child or youth (as defined in ), a runaway, in foster care or has aged out of the foster care system, a child eligible for assistance under , or in an out-of-home placement.section 12473(6) of title 34section 11434a(2) of title 421section 677 of title 42So in original. Probably should be preceded by “who”.1

children.label: VI
children.text: An individual who is pregnant or parenting.

children.label: VII
children.text: A youth who is an individual with a disability.

children.label: VIII
children.text: A low-income individual who requires additional assistance to enter or complete an educational program or to secure or hold employment.

children.label: i
children.text: attending school (as defined by State law);

children.label: ii
children.text: not younger than age 14 or (unless an individual with a disability who is attending school under State law) older than age 21;

children.label: iii
children.text: a low-income individual; and

children.label: I
children.text: Basic skills deficient.

children.label: II
children.text: An English language learner.

children.label: III
children.text: An offender.

children.label: IV
children.text: A homeless individual (as defined in ), a homeless child or youth (as defined in ), a runaway, in foster care or has aged out of the foster care system, a child eligible for assistance under , or in an out-of-home placement.section 12473(6) of title 34section 11434a(2) of title 42section 677 of title 421

children.label: V
children.text: Pregnant or parenting.

children.label: VI
children.text: A youth who is an individual with a disability.

children.label: VII
children.text: An individual who requires additional assistance to complete an educational program or to secure or hold employment.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 2 Special rule
children.text: For the purpose of this subsection, the term “low-income”, used with respect to an individual, also includes a youth living in a high-poverty area.

children.heading: Exception for persons who are not low-income individuals
children.label: A Exception for persons who are not low-income individuals

children.heading: Definition
children.label: i Definition
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “covered individual” means an in-school youth, or an out-of-school youth who is described in subclause (III) or (VIII) of paragraph (1)(B)(iii).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: In each local area, not more than 5 percent of the individuals assisted under this section may be persons who would be covered individuals, except that the persons are not low-income individuals.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: In each local area, not more than 5 percent of the in-school youth assisted under this section may be eligible under paragraph (1) because the youth are in-school youth described in paragraph (1)(C)(iv)(VII).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For any program year, not less than 75 percent of the funds allotted under , reserved under , and available for statewide activities under subsection (b), and not less than 75 percent of funds available to local areas under subsection (c), shall be used to provide youth workforce investment activities for out-of-school youth.section 3162(b)(1)(C) of this titlesection 3163(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: after an analysis of the in-school youth and out-of-school youth populations in the local area, the State determines that the local area will be unable to use at least 75 percent of the funds available for activities under subsection (c) to serve out-of-school youth due to a low number of out-of-school youth; and

children.label: I
children.text: the State submits to the Secretary, for the local area, a request including a proposed percentage decreased to not less than 50 percent for purposes of subparagraph (A), and a summary of the analysis described in clause (i); and

children.label: II
children.text: the request is approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Consistency with compulsory school attendance laws
children.label: 5 Consistency with compulsory school attendance laws
children.text: In providing assistance under this section to an individual who is required to attend school under applicable State compulsory school attendance laws, the priority in providing such assistance shall be for the individual to attend school regularly.

children.label: A
children.text: conducting evaluations under of activities authorized under this subpart and subpart 3 in coordination with evaluations carried out by the Secretary under ;section 3141(e) of this titlesection 3224(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: disseminating a list of eligible providers of youth workforce investment activities, as determined under ;section 3153 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: providing assistance to local areas as described in subsections (b)(7) and (c)(2) of , for local coordination of activities carried out under this subchapter;section 3121 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: operating a fiscal and management accountability information system under ;section 3141(i) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: carrying out monitoring and oversight of activities carried out under this subpart and subpart 3, which may include a review comparing the services provided to male and female youth; and

children.label: F
children.text: providing additional assistance to local areas that have high concentrations of eligible youth.

children.label: i
children.text: research related to meeting the education and employment needs of eligible youth; and

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstration projects related to meeting the education and employment needs of eligible youth;

children.label: B
children.text: supporting the development of alternative, evidence-based programs and other activities that enhance the choices available to eligible youth and encourage such youth to reenter and complete secondary education, enroll in postsecondary education and advanced training, progress through a career pathway, and enter into unsubsidized employment that leads to economic self-sufficiency;

children.label: C
children.text: supporting the provision of career services described in in the one-stop delivery system in the State;section 3174(c)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: supporting the ability of participants to create household budgets, initiate savings plans, and make informed financial decisions about education, retirement, home ownership, wealth building, or other savings goals;

children.label: ii
children.text: supporting the ability to manage spending, credit, and debt, including credit card debt, effectively;

children.label: iii
children.text: increasing awareness of the availability and significance of credit reports and credit scores in obtaining credit, including determining their accuracy (and how to correct inaccuracies in the reports and scores), and their effect on credit terms;

children.label: iv
children.text: supporting the ability to understand, evaluate, and compare financial products, services, and opportunities; and

children.label: v
children.text: supporting activities that address the particular financial literacy needs of non-English speakers, including providing the support through the development and distribution of multilingual financial literacy and education materials; and

children.label: E
children.text: providing technical assistance to, as appropriate, local boards, chief elected officials, one-stop operators, one-stop partners, and eligible providers, in local areas, which provision of technical assistance shall include the development and training of staff, the development of exemplary program activities, the provision of technical assistance to local areas that fail to meet local performance accountability measures described in , and the provision of technology to facilitate remote access to services provided through the one-stop delivery system in the State.section 3141(c) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: Not more than 5 percent of the funds allotted to a State under shall be used by the State for administrative activities carried out under this subsection or .section 3162(b)(1)(C) of this titlesection 3174(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: provide an objective assessment of the academic levels, skill levels, and service needs of each participant, which assessment shall include a review of basic skills, occupational skills, prior work experience, employability, interests, aptitudes (including interests and aptitudes for nontraditional jobs), supportive service needs, and developmental needs of such participant, for the purpose of identifying appropriate services and career pathways for participants, except that a new assessment of a participant is not required if the provider carrying out such a program determines it is appropriate to use a recent assessment of the participant conducted pursuant to another education or training program;

children.label: B
children.text: develop service strategies for each participant that are directly linked to 1 or more of the indicators of performance described in , and that shall identify career pathways that include education and employment goals (including, in appropriate circumstances, nontraditional employment), appropriate achievement objectives, and appropriate services for the participant taking into account the assessment conducted pursuant to subparagraph (A), except that a new service strategy for a participant is not required if the provider carrying out such a program determines it is appropriate to use a recent service strategy developed for the participant under another education or training program;section 3141(b)(2)(A)(ii) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: activities leading to the attainment of a secondary school diploma or its recognized equivalent, or a recognized postsecondary credential;

children.label: ii
children.text: preparation for postsecondary educational and training opportunities;

children.label: iii
children.text: strong linkages between academic instruction (based on challenging State academic standards established under and occupational education that lead to the attainment of recognized postsecondary credentials;section 6311(b)(1) of title 202So in original. Probably should be followed by a closing parenthesis.2

children.label: iv
children.text: preparation for unsubsidized employment opportunities, in appropriate cases; and

children.label: v
children.text: effective connections to employers, including small employers, in in-demand industry sectors and occupations of the local and regional labor markets; and

children.label: D
children.text: at the discretion of the local board, implement a pay-for-performance contract strategy for elements described in paragraph (2), for which the local board may reserve and use not more than 10 percent of the total funds allocated to the local area under .section 3163(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: tutoring, study skills training, instruction, and evidence-based dropout prevention and recovery strategies that lead to completion of the requirements for a secondary school diploma or its recognized equivalent (including a recognized certificate of attendance or similar document for individuals with disabilities) or for a recognized postsecondary credential;

children.label: B
children.text: alternative secondary school services, or dropout recovery services, as appropriate;

children.label: i
children.text: summer employment opportunities and other employment opportunities available throughout the school year;

children.label: ii
children.text: pre-apprenticeship programs;

children.label: iii
children.text: internships and job shadowing; and

children.label: iv
children.text: on-the-job training opportunities;

children.label: D
children.text: occupational skill training, which shall include priority consideration for training programs that lead to recognized postsecondary credentials that are aligned with in-demand industry sectors or occupations in the local area involved, if the local board determines that the programs meet the quality criteria described in ;section 3153 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: education offered concurrently with and in the same context as workforce preparation activities and training for a specific occupation or occupational cluster;

children.label: F
children.text: leadership development opportunities, which may include community service and peer-centered activities encouraging responsibility and other positive social and civic behaviors, as appropriate;

children.label: G
children.text: supportive services;

children.label: H
children.text: adult mentoring for the period of participation and a subsequent period, for a total of not less than 12 months;

children.label: I
children.text: followup services for not less than 12 months after the completion of participation, as appropriate;

children.label: J
children.text: comprehensive guidance and counseling, which may include drug and alcohol abuse counseling and referral, as appropriate;

children.label: K
children.text: financial literacy education;

children.label: L
children.text: entrepreneurial skills training;

children.label: M
children.text: services that provide labor market and employment information about in-demand industry sectors or occupations available in the local area, such as career awareness, career counseling, and career exploration services; and

children.label: N
children.text: activities that help youth prepare for and transition to postsecondary education and training.

children.label: i
children.text: information on the full array of applicable or appropriate services that are available through the local board or other eligible providers or one-stop partners, including those providers or partners receiving funds under this part; and

children.label: ii
children.text: referral to appropriate training and educational programs that have the capacity to serve the participant either on a sequential or concurrent basis.

children.heading: Applicants not meeting enrollment requirements
children.label: B Applicants not meeting enrollment requirements
children.text: Each eligible provider of a program of youth workforce investment activities shall ensure that an eligible applicant who does not meet the enrollment requirements of the particular program or who cannot be served shall be referred for further assessment, as necessary, and referred to appropriate programs in accordance with subparagraph (A) to meet the basic skills and training needs of the applicant.

children.heading: Involvement in design and implementation
children.label: C Involvement in design and implementation
children.text: The local board shall ensure that parents, participants, and other members of the community with experience relating to programs for youth are involved in the design and implementation of the programs described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 4 Priority
children.text: Not less than 20 percent of the funds allocated to the local area as described in paragraph (1) shall be used to provide in-school youth and out-of-school youth with activities under paragraph (2)(C).

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 5 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subpart shall be construed to require that each of the elements described in subparagraphs of paragraph (2) be offered by each provider of youth services.

children.heading: Prohibition against Federal control of education
children.label: A Prohibition against Federal control of education
children.text: No provision of this Act shall be construed to authorize any department, agency, officer, or employee of the United States to exercise any direction, supervision, or control over the curriculum, program of instruction, administration, or personnel of any educational institution, school, or school system, or over the selection of library resources, textbooks, or other printed or published instructional materials by any educational institution, school, or school system.

children.heading: Noninterference and nonreplacement of regular academic requirements
children.label: B Noninterference and nonreplacement of regular academic requirements
children.text: No funds described in paragraph (1) shall be used to provide an activity for eligible youth who are not school dropouts if participation in the activity would interfere with or replace the regular academic requirements of the youth.

children.heading: Linkages
children.label: 7 Linkages
children.text: In coordinating the programs authorized under this section, local boards shall establish linkages with local educational agencies responsible for services to participants as appropriate.

children.heading: Volunteers
children.label: 8 Volunteers
children.text: The local board shall make opportunities available for individuals who have successfully participated in programs carried out under this section to volunteer assistance to participants in the form of mentoring, tutoring, and other activities.
